items.title: The war in Iran has left hundreds of U.S. diplomats in limbo
items.description: As the U.S-Iran war ramps up again, hundreds of diplomats are in limbo. The State Department drew down staffing due to Iran's attacks on Gulf states. It's unclear if they'll return to normal business.

items.title: At least 930 people have died in Congo's Ebola outbreak
items.description: Thirty-seven new deaths were recorded in the previous 24 hours in one of the highest single-day totals

items.title: Hasta el final: What propels Argentina's comeback kings
items.description: A string of dramatic comebacks and extra-time victories fuels confidence that the Albiceleste will fight until the very end – and succeed. Leer más

items.title: Cameroon: President Paul Biya’s prolonged absence raises questions
items.description: Described as one of the longest-serving presidents in Africa, Cameroonian President Paul Biya's absence from the country continues to draw criticism.

items.title: Seized scam centre in Myanmar offers rare glimpse into industry targeting Australians
items.description: The scam industry in Myanmar is morphing and heading into the jungle rather than disappearing since a crackdown began.

items.title: US looks to speed air taxis, supersonic planes to market
From Maradona to Messi: Bangladesh's enduring love for Argentina
items.description: The craze for Argentina has endured for decades in Bangladesh, a country of 170 million people who have never qualified for the World Cup. Leer más

items.title: Extreme heat and weather conditions attributed to stagnant jet stream
items.description: It’s no coincidence that extreme heat is engulfing huge swaths of Asia, Europe and North America all at the same time.

items.title: France faces calls to raise detention of Gabon opposition figure during Nguema visit
items.description: Pressure is mounting on France to address the detention of Gabonese opposition figure and former Prime Minister Alain-Claude Bilie-By-Nze as President Brice Oligui Nguema begins a three-day state visit to Paris.

items.title: Messi on eve of final: Argentina has already written history 'no-one can erase'
items.description: Argentina captain says the team's greatest achievement has been the journey rather than the trophies as he delivers an emotional message to fans before Sunday's World Cup final. Leer más

items.title: Ten killed as Russian attacks on merchant ships in Black Sea intensify
items.description: Russian strikes on the Odesa region have killed 28 people in July alone, local authorities say.
